feedback.text: We all would love and hope to get your help with this issue of our digital ownership and protect our rights. In the past, before the digital product existed, people owned their games. Now the big game companies are trying to prevent that by saying that consumers have bought a license (without an expiration date) and not a product that you own. Therefore, big companies like Nintendo, EA or Ubisoft have/can destroy, remove the product or lock access to anyone who wants to use their games (they can do this for no reason if they want to, it's in their EULA). Many people who have lost their games have also not gotten their money back. We EU citizens who love games want you to help us maintain/protect consumer law and digital ownership. In Brazil they are trying to protect their population's consumer law which Nintendo has violated by disabling the Nintendo Switch 2 function from their offices (remote). If someone had bought an electric car. Then the company would not like the customer and would have locked them out of using their car and maybe even made them drive it back to the company. Then also think if they didn't give the money back either. That's how it feels for many gamers. The money doesn't matter that much we want to keep our games/car because we like the product and have many memories of it. Companies refuse to make single-player games and will only do everything with live service with microtransactions and payments to make money, but when it doesn't go well or is no longer profitable, they close the game and burn the product so we can't use it again. And if they make a bad game with low effort they blame us for not buying or it for not being a live service. If buying isn't owning piracy isn't stealing is a quote that has been created because of these gaming companies. The links are to diffrent articles and Youtube videos that cover this topic and are mentioned in the text above. Brazil lawsuit aginst Nintendo: https://www.gamespot.com/articles/brazil-takes-issue-with-nintendo-bricking-switch-2s/1100-6533039/ Ubisoft EULA wants us to destroy our games YouTube: https://youtu.be/EDDrbKUHEmQ?si=-o3ERr-GrHKsGDgj EA (Q and A) shutting down their game and will not be playable after 2026: https://www.ea.com/games/anthem/news/anthem-game-update?isLocalized=true 27,000 games gone from steam YouTube: https://youtu.be/IlEHovdeKZw?si=W0O2dQyFrPwZXQbA Extrem group demands mature games to not exist YouTube: https://youtu.be/ao9c2iVfPQc?si=gDBWIyMe7yC4TaUQ Concord live service game died within 14 days of relese YouTube: https://youtu.be/O8Z3B0N34WY?si=dFfRIKdmecMYCBmM EA CEO blames us and the game for not being a live service (from a singelplayer games 4 instalment) YouTube and article: https://youtu.be/tYNCKUTTI-k?si=YGYY6TmZ4AH8HKRt https://nordic.ign.com/dragon-age-dreadwolf/91036/news/ea-ceo-says-dragon-age-the-veilguard-failed-to-resonate-with-a-broad-audience-gamers-increasingly-wa
